crash.title: Something went wrong
crash.body: Echo hit an unexpected error. Reloading the view usually fixes it.

perm.needsAccessibility: Auto-paste needs Accessibility access. Your text is on the clipboard — enable Echo under System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility.

intro.accountBody: With your subunit account you get cloud transcription — GDPR-compliant, servers in Germany. Without an account, Echo runs fully on-device.
intro.accountSkipHint: You can do this later in Settings.
intro.micBody: Say something — the waves are your voice.
intro.micNoSignal: No signal yet — pick another microphone or move closer.
intro.hotkeyBody: Echo listens while you hold your hotkey. Type a few keys — the keyboard lights up with you.
intro.hotkeyChangeable: You can change it any time in Settings.
intro.modeBody: Local keeps everything on your device. Cloud is more accurate — GDPR-compliant in Germany.
intro.finaleHint2: and speak — release when you're done.
intro.finaleSuccess: That's how it works — everywhere, in any app.
intro.finaleNothingHeard: Nothing heard — keep holding the keys while you speak.
intro.finaleSkipHint: No problem — you're ready to go.

app.comingSoon: Coming in one of the next phases.

help.subtitle: Answers, an assistant and diagnostics — all right inside Echo.
help.askSub: Ask in your own words. The assistant answers based on Echo's help.
help.askError: The assistant isn't reachable right now. Check the FAQ below or email us at {{email}}.

suggest.paste: My text isn't being pasted — what do I do?
suggest.hotkey: How do I change the hotkey?
suggest.meeting: How do I record a meeting?

help.diagSub: When something breaks, diagnostics help us find the cause fast.

notes.title: Notes
notes.sub: Speak notes or type them — always in sync with your iPhone.
notes.empty: No notes yet. Record or type one — or speak it on your iPhone while out, and it shows up here.
notes.folderIsEmpty: This folder is empty. Recordings land right here.
notes.deleteMsg: This note will be removed on all your devices.
notes.folderCopied: Copied all notes in the folder
notes.folderEmpty: The folder has no notes to copy.
notes.deleteFolderMsg: The notes are kept — they're just removed from the folder.

record.errorTrialExpired: Trial expired — please upgrade your plan.
record.errorNotSignedIn: Not signed in — sign in from Settings.
record.errorModelMissing: Local engine unavailable — switch to Cloud.

models.notDownloaded: not downloaded — click to download + use

hotkey.noModifierWarning: Without a modifier (Ctrl/Alt/…) the hotkey fires on every keystroke.
hotkey.pickerBody: Press a key combo — or click / hold a single key (e.g. Control or Option) to dictate.
hotkey.holdDurationHint: How long the single key must be held before dictation starts. A short tap does nothing — the key stays usable as normal.
hotkey.permNeeded: Single-key hotkeys need the “Input Monitoring” permission. Enable it for Echo in System Settings (a restart may be needed).

update.restartHint: Echo will restart automatically in a moment.
update.oneClickHint: · one click, the rest is automatic.

session.expired: Your session has expired. Please sign in again to keep using cloud transcription.

home.title: Push & speak
home.emptyHistory: No transcriptions yet — hit your hotkey and get going.

history.title: History
history.latencyHint: Latency: recording stop until text inserted

meetings.title: Meetings
meetings.liveDesc: Start a meeting or join one — automatic minutes & clean speaker separation. Each device records its own audio.
meetings.subtitle: Long recordings (≥ {{minutes}} min) — stored separately. Click to open the full transcript; the buttons generate summary / action items / decisions.

vocab.title: Vocabulary
vocab.subtitle: Custom terms bias Whisper and fix recurring mishearings. „Sounds like“ → becomes „written as“.
vocab.enabledSub: Replaces your terms on every dictation — independent of whether cleanup is on or off.
vocab.suggestEmpty: No suggestions yet — Echo learns from your history and surfaces recurring mishearings here.
vocab.clearAutoHint: Removes every automatically learned word from the dictionary.
vocab.autoSub: Echo spots recurring terms that get transcribed inconsistently and asks for the correct spelling — confident matches are learned silently.

settings.title: Settings
settings.hotkeyHint: Click, then press a key combination
settings.targetLockHint: Paste into the last focused window
settings.showBubbleHint: Compact status indicator when the orb overlay is off
settings.soundsHint: A short tone on record start and on paste.
settings.soundPasteHint: Tone when the finished text is pasted.
settings.glassStrengthHint: How strong the frosted-glass effect is — Off = flat, no blur.
settings.uiScaleHint: Scales the whole interface — smaller = compact module. The window can be dragged down to match.
settings.localModelHint: Click to select + download the model (go big with a GPU — large-v3-turbo).
settings.transcriptionLanguageHint: A fixed language is fastest. Use "Auto-detect" only for mixed languages — it costs ~½ s extra per dictation (detection pass).
settings.cloudQualityHint: Quality = fast (V3 Turbo). Highest quality = best accuracy (full V3); the final pass on release takes a few seconds longer.
settings.instantLiveTypingHint: Instant mode only: type the finished result in progressively instead of pasting all at once. Off = instant paste (Ctrl+V, atomic, reliable — recommended on Windows).
settings.instantLiveTypingLiveNote: Disabled: live streaming already types as you speak — having both on would double up.
settings.streamingLiveTapNote: Tip: live typing is most reliable with toggle activation (tap to start/stop). In hold mode the hotkey stays pressed, which can suppress live typing in some apps (especially terminals/editors on Windows).
settings.deCommasHint: Inserts missing commas before German subordinate clauses (dass, weil, wenn …) and repairs “gesagt das er” → “gesagt, dass er” — rule-based, zero latency
settings.fillerRemovalHint: Strips „äh/ähm/hmm“ automatically — deterministic, no added latency (no AI call)
settings.aiCleanupHint: Full AI polish — smooths the text more, but costs a server call (some latency)
settings.cleanupStyleHint: Standard = standard spelling & punctuation correction (local, GDPR-safe)
settings.autoModeHint: Style automatically by active window — now also by browser tab (ChatGPT/Cursor→Prompt, Gmail→Email, Slack→Slack, Docs/Notion→Formal)
settings.customRulesHint: Window title contains "text" → style. Overrides the built-in rules.
settings.orbConfiguratorHint: Live preview — every setting below takes effect instantly up here.
settings.orbPresetHint: Ready-made palettes — picking one sets all three state colors.
settings.orbProfilesSynced: Saved to your account — synced across your devices.
settings.orbProfilesLocalOnly: Saved on this device. Sign in to sync across devices.
settings.orbProfilesEmpty: No profiles yet. Style your orb above, then save it as a profile.
settings.idleModeHint: How the orb behaves when nothing's happening.
settings.orbTriggerHint: Whether the orb functions appear on hover, or only when you click the orb. In click mode, clicking the orb again hides them.
settings.idleAnimationHint: Gentle motion at rest. Off = the orb stays still and only reacts to real speech.
settings.orbAppearHint: Animation when the orb materializes — after launch and whenever it returns from hidden.
settings.pillColorModeHint: How much color the pill shows: state colors, colorless at rest only — or always pure liquid glass, where motion alone tells the state.
settings.pillReactionHint: How the pill's bars react to your voice. Dynamics: every bar with its own character and tempo. Classic: strong centre with deep dynamics.
settings.pillConfiguratorHint: Live preview of the pill — everything below takes effect instantly.
settings.pillNotActiveHint: These options shape the pill. Your overlay is currently using a different style.
settings.pillVisualHint: What plays inside the pill while you speak. Standard keeps the familiar pill — the rest is taste.
settings.pillVisualLaufband: Ticker — speech runs through from right to left
settings.pillVisualZentrum: Center — peaks drift outward from the middle
settings.pillVisualWelle: Wave — one continuous glowing waveform ribbon
settings.pillVisualMatrix: Matrix — discrete light dots, retro VU
settings.pillVisualWorte: Words — your dictation flows through as text
settings.pillVisualPuls: Pulse — sonar rings run out from the centre
settings.pillVisualHelix: Helix — a double wave that opens with your voice
settings.pillWordsDemo: Echo types what you speak — fast precise and everywhere
settings.pillGlowHint: Makes the glass itself light up and react to your voice — like a Siri pill.
settings.pillGlowStatus: Status color — light follows your voice
settings.pillGlowSiri: Siri — multicolor aurora, hues follow your tone
settings.accountHint: Sign in with auth.subunit.ai for cloud transcription
settings.signOutConfirmMessage: You'll be signed out of your Subunit account and will need to sign in again to use cloud transcription.
settings.manageAccountHint: Opens your Subunit account page in the browser
settings.loginFailed: Sign-in failed or was cancelled. Please try again.
settings.saveToSynapseHint: Write transcripts to the knowledge base
settings.updateInstalling: Installing… Echo will restart automatically in a moment.
settings.replayIntroHint: Watch the app's first-run introduction again.
settings.promptHotkeyHint: Global shortcut that shows/hides the floating Prompt Terminal
settings.promptAsTargetHint: Every transcription lands in the Prompt Terminal instead of the active app
settings.promptFallbackHint: No text field focused? Your dictation automatically lands in the Prompt Console instead of vanishing
settings.promptBlurHint: On: the terminal smoothly switches to the real iOS frost on open. Off: it stays in the flat, pill-matched glass throughout — no switch
settings.promptThemeHint: Dark or light prompt terminal — each in the pill's color tone
settings.soundStopHint: Plays when you release the key (end of recording) — choose from three tones. Independently toggleable.
settings.liveTypingHint: Types the text into the target window while you speak. Off = the full result is pasted when you release (recommended, fastest mode).

meetLocal.cardDesc: Recording, transcript & speaker separation entirely on this device — your audio never leaves the machine. For pod meetings with one microphone.
meetLocal.title: Local meeting
meetLocal.pitch: The entire meeting pipeline runs on this device: Whisper transcription, voice check-in and speaker separation — even fully offline. Audio never leaves the machine.
meetLocal.gateBuilt: App build includes the local meet backend
meetLocal.modelHint: On first start the voice model (~26 MB) is downloaded — everything runs offline afterwards.
meetLocal.participantsEmpty: No participants yet — add names, then each person reads their number out loud so Echo learns the voices.
meetLocal.checkinPrompt: {{name}}, please read this number out loud:
meetLocal.checkinHint: Just speak towards the microphone at normal volume — about 8 seconds.
meetLocal.checkinFailed: Couldn't recognize {{name}}'s number — just click check-in again.
meetLocal.processingHint: The remaining audio is being transcribed and assigned to speakers. Depending on length this can take a few minutes — all locally.
meetLocal.gateBuiltOk: Pipeline on board: whisper.cpp + voiceprint model (ONNX)
meetLocal.gateBuiltFail: This build does not include the local pipeline
meetLocal.hwWhySilicon: Apple Silicon detected — transcription and speaker separation run smoothly right on the chip.
meetLocal.hwWhyRam: {{ram}} GB RAM — enough headroom to run Whisper and speaker separation in parallel.
meetLocal.hwWhyFail: Only {{ram}} GB RAM and no Apple Silicon — the local pipeline (Whisper + voiceprints) would be noticeably too slow on this device.
meetLocal.verdictOk: Assessment: your device is ready. Recording, transcription and speaker separation run fully offline — audio never leaves the device.
meetLocal.verdictFail: Assessment: a local meeting isn't sensible on this device — the cloud variant will deliver the better result here.

prompt.title: Prompt Terminal
prompt.libraryEmpty: No saved prompts yet — save your first draft.
prompt.tabHint: Double-click: rename · Right-click: menu · Drag: reorder
prompt.targetHint: Terminal as target: dictations land here instead of the app

palette.placeholder: Type a command or jump to a tab/library entry …

correct.hint: Correct the whole text once (typos, punctuation) — wording kept
correct.failed: Correction failed — check connection/login and retry.

mic.start: Dictate — click to record (no hotkey to hold)
mic.failed: Recording failed — needs cloud mode + login.

refine.hint: Rewrite into a clean, well-structured prompt via AI
refine.title: AI-refined
refine.failed: Refine failed — check connection/login and retry.

coach.title: Prompt Coach
coach.allGood: Strong — your prompt covers every context building block.

q.goal: What exactly should the AI do? Describe the task in at least one full sentence.
q.role: What role should the AI take? An expert in what?
q.context: What background matters? Give context about the situation.
q.format: What should the result look like? List, table, length …
q.audience: Who is the result for? Name the audience.
q.examples: Show an example of what the result should look like.
q.constraints: What should the AI avoid? Set limits: style, tone, length.

tpl.role: Role: You are an experienced expert in
tpl.format: Format: answer as a list/table/prose, at most … words.
tpl.examples: Example — the result should look like this:

activity.title: Activity
activity.subtitle: Your dictation activity over time.
activity.wordCloudEmpty: Not enough words yet to build a picture.
activity.empty: No activity yet. Start dictating to see your stats here.
activity.partialRangeHint: Day-level data only exists since {{since}} — {{words}} words from {{count}} earlier dictations are only included in “All time”.

learning.title: Learning
learning.subtitle: How your vocabulary is developing.
learning.ttrHint: Share of unique words relative to total words — the higher, the more varied your language.
learning.fillerSub: What Echo filters out for you — and what it deliberately leaves in.
learning.fillerRemovedSub: Echo removed these hesitation sounds from your dictations automatically — they never made it into the final text.
learning.fillerKeptSub: Echo leaves these fillers standing — cutting them would change what you actually said. Your verbal crutches.
learning.upgradeSub: Frequently used words with stronger alternatives
learning.upgradeHint: Frequently used words with stronger alternatives — tap or hover a word for its explanation.
learning.empty: No data yet. Dictate more to unlock your learning insights.
learning.needMoreData: Dictate a bit more to unlock this insight.
learning.wodChallenge: Use it in a dictation · +{{xp}} XP
learning.lbSub: Who is growing their vocabulary the most this week?
learning.rewardWodToast: Word of the day used: “{{word}}” · +{{xp}} XP
learning.topWordsSub: Your most frequent words in the selected period.
learning.distinctiveSub: Rare, personal terms Echo should always spell correctly — add them to your vocabulary.
learning.dexSub: Rare words you have actually spoken in real dictations — collected like a Pokédex.
learning.dexEmptyHint: Speak rare, unusual words in your dictations — every new one lands here in your Wortdex, with its rarity tier, number and first-sighting sentence.
learning.achSub: Milestones you unlock as you collect and practise — each grants a wearable title.

wod_7.name: Word-of-Day Week
wod_7.desc: Use the word of the day on 7 days.

wod_30.name: Word-of-Day Month
wod_30.desc: Use the word of the day on 30 days.

speech.overallSub: How you speak – analysed deterministically and locally.
speech.noGhost: First reading – from now on you'll see your progress.
speech.emptyHint: Dictate ~500 words and Echo will draw your speech profile. So far: {{count}} words.
speech.dimSub: Each dimension rolls several metrics into one score (0–100).
speech.insightsSub: Concrete pointers, drawn from your most recent changes.

variety.name: Variety
variety.info: How varied your vocabulary is – measures range and the share of rare, elevated words.

precision.name: Precision
precision.info: How precisely you phrase things – rewards concrete terms, penalises weak, vague and hedging words.

clarity.name: Clarity
clarity.info: How easy your sentences are to follow – from sentence length, readability and nesting.

structure.name: Structure
structure.info: How well you connect ideas – measures the amount and variety of your linking words.

active.name: Active voice
active.info: How active and concrete your style is – little passive and little nominal style raise the score.

fluency.name: Fluency
fluency.info: How fluently you speak – from fillers, pace stability and sentence rhythm.

mtld.name: Vocabulary variety (MTLD)
mtld.info: Measures how long you speak before your vocabulary repeats. Higher is better – independent of text length.

eleganceRate.name: Elevated words
eleganceRate.info: Share of sophisticated, precise terms per 1000 words. More of them reads as more refined.

distinctWords.name: Distinct words
distinctWords.info: Number of different words in the analysed window.

hapaxRate.name: One-off words
hapaxRate.info: Share of words you used exactly once – a signal of vocabulary breadth.

weakRate.name: Filler nouns
weakRate.info: Pale catch-all words like “thing”, “stuff”, “do” per 1000 words. Fewer is more precise.

vagueRate.name: Vague terms
vagueRate.info: Fuzzy words like “somehow”, “actually”, “sort of” per 1000 words. Fewer sounds more definite.

hedgeRate.name: Hedging phrases
hedgeRate.info: Qualifiers like “I think”, “maybe”, “probably” per 1000 words. Fewer sounds more assured.

lix.name: Readability index
lix.info: Readability from sentence length and share of long words. Lower means easier to follow.

nestedShare.name: Nested sentences
nestedShare.info: Share of sentences with deep sub-clause nesting. Fewer is clearer.

avgSentence.name: Avg. sentence length
avgSentence.info: Average words per sentence. Shorter sentences are usually easier to understand.

p90Sentence.name: Long sentences (p90)
p90Sentence.info: Length of your longest 10% of sentences (in words). Upward outliers make listening harder.

connDensity.name: Linking words
connDensity.info: Density of logical connectors like “because”, “yet”, “therefore” per sentence. More structure is better.

connBuckets.name: Connector variety
connBuckets.info: How many different kinds of linking words you use (causal, contrastive, temporal …).

passiveShare.name: Passive share
passiveShare.info: Share of sentences in the passive voice. Active sentences feel more direct and alive.

nominalRate.name: Nominal style
nominalRate.info: Nominalised verbs (‑tion, ‑ment …) per 1000 words. Fewer of them sounds more concrete.

fillerRate.name: Fillers
fillerRate.info: Hesitations like “uh”, “like”, “basically” per 1000 words. Fewer reads as more assured.

wpmCv.name: Pace stability
wpmCv.info: How even your speaking pace is. Lower means calmer and more controlled.

rhythmSd.name: Sentence rhythm
rhythmSd.info: How much your sentence lengths vary. Some variation keeps listening engaging.

hedging_up.tip: Cut “I think”, “maybe”, “somehow” – state the point directly.

weak_up.tip: Swap “thing”, “stuff”, “do” for the precise word you actually mean.

filler_up.tip: Pause instead of filling – a beat of silence beats an “uh”.

filler_down.tip: Keep it up: deliberate pauses keep your delivery calm and clear.

passive_up.tip: Go active: “We decide this” instead of “It is decided”.

nesting_up.tip: Break up long sentences – one idea per sentence is easier to follow.

variety_up.tip: Stay curious – new words from the Wortdex lock in the effect.

elegance_up.tip: Use elevated terms deliberately – impact comes from choice, not volume.

monotone_rhythm.finding: Your sentence rhythm has been very uniform lately.
monotone_rhythm.tip: Alternate short and long sentences on purpose – it creates tension for the listener.

connectors_low.finding: You use noticeably few linking words.
connectors_low.tip: Connect ideas explicitly: “because”, “yet”, “therefore” guide your audience.

loop.packEmptyIntro: Seven words, curated for how you speak — each with its meaning, an example and a personal reason it's for you. Have this week's pack put together.
loop.packCurating: ~40 seconds — your pack is being curated
loop.packError: Curation failed — your existing pack stays.
loop.dueSub: Use them in a real dictation — it earns XP and levels them up.
loop.ownSub: How firmly the taught words are already yours.
loop.ownLegend: Used: spoken at least once in a real dictation. Fortified: repeated across several days. Mastered: firmly anchored over many days.

dojo.title: Rhetoric Dojo
dojo.sub: One spoken micro-workout a day — record, instant verdict, XP.

gauntlet.name: Filler Gauntlet
gauntlet.rule: Speak freely about the topic — without a single filler word.

tabu.name: Taboo
tabu.rule: Explain the term without using any of the taboo words.

better.name: Say It Better
better.rule: Reword the sentence more powerfully — away from weak and vague words.

dojo.doneTodayHint: You may practice again — but there's no further XP for it today.

result.title: Your result
result.tooShort: Too short to score fairly — speak longer next time.

quests.title: Your weekly quests
quests.sub: Fresh every week — three goals, one full bar.

workouts_3.name: Three workouts
workouts_3.desc: Complete three dojo exercises this week.

coach_5.name: Five coach words in the wild
coach_5.desc: Use five recommended words in real dictations.

find_1.name: One new find
find_1.desc: Collect a new rare word in the Wortdex.

vp.introHint: Store your voice once — meetings will recognise you automatically (no number check-in) and your profile can keep refining itself with every dictation and meeting.
vp.reenrollHint: Our recognition model was updated — please re-record once.
vp.setupHint: Guided recording (~60 seconds of read-aloud text) — the quieter the room, the better the core.
vp.adaptiveHint: Uses excerpts from your own meetings and dictations to automatically refine your profile. Separate purpose, separate consent — switch off anytime.
vp.resetConfirmText: All automatically learned anchors will be deleted. Your core setup stays; with learning enabled the profile rebuilds from fresh data.
vp.deleteHint: Withdraws consent — meetings fall back to the number check-in.
vp.deleteConfirmText: Your voiceprint and all learned anchors will be removed. Meetings will use the number check-in again.
vp.consentText: Your voiceprint is biometric data. It is stored encrypted on our servers in Germany, used exclusively for speaker recognition in your meetings and never shared with third parties. You can delete it here at any time.
vp.consentCheck: I explicitly consent to Subunit storing my voiceprint for speaker recognition (Art. 9 GDPR).
vp.readAloud: Read the text aloud at normal volume — about one minute:
vp.readText1: Hello, I'm setting up my voice for Subunit Echo. This way I'll be recognised automatically in meetings and won't have to read a check-in number anymore.
vp.readText2: In everyday work I discuss projects, appointments and decisions. Seven, three, eight, two, nine — numbers like these come up just like names, places and technical terms.
vp.readText3: Finally a calm sentence at normal volume: I simply speak the way I would in a meeting — not faster, not slower.
vp.speakUp: Start speaking — the dot shows your level.
vp.errLogin: Please sign in with your Subunit account first (Account tab).
vp.errLoad: Could not load status — please try again later.
vp.errBusy: Another recording is running — please wait a moment.
vp.errMic: Could not start the microphone — check the device in settings.
vp.figFromTimbre: Drawn from the sound of your voice
vp.figFromVoice: Drawn from your voice · your tone profile follows with your next dictation
